messages.text: :red_circle: *[ALERT - P1]* auth-service error rate exceeded 5% threshold (currently 8.3%) over 5-minute window. Triggered at 2026-03-15 08:00:00 UTC.

text.text: :red_circle: *[ALERT - P1]* auth-service error rate exceeded 5% threshold (currently 8.3%) over 5-minute window. Triggered at 2026-03-15 08:00:00 UTC.

attachments.title: P1 Alert: auth-service error rate > 5%
attachments.text: Error rate is 8.3% (threshold: 5%). Affected endpoints: POST /auth/token (12.1% errors), GET /auth/validate (6.7% errors). Last healthy: 2026-03-15 07:54:00 UTC.

messages.text: I'm seeing this too - auth latency spiked about 10 minutes ago. Checking the token validation service now. @usr_gamma can you pull the DB connection stats?

text.text: I'm seeing this too - auth latency spiked about 10 minutes ago. Checking the token validation service now. @usr_gamma can you pull the DB connection stats?

messages.text: DB connections look fine - pool at 45/100. But I see something weird: the JWT verify function is throwing `key not found` errors for tokens issued after midnight. Looks like the key rotation cron job ran but the in-memory key cache wasn't invalidated.

text.text: DB connections look fine - pool at 45/100. But I see something weird: the JWT verify function is throwing `key not found` errors for tokens issued after midnight. Looks like the key rotation cron job ran but the in-memory key cache wasn't invalidated.

messages.text:
Root cause confirmed: `TokenKeyCache.Refresh()` is only called on startup. When key rotation ran at 00:00 UTC and replaced the signing keys, the cache still held the old public keys. All tokens issued after rotation fail validation.

Fix: add a cache TTL (suggest 5min) and hook the rotation event to trigger an async refresh. PR incoming.

text.text:
Root cause confirmed: `TokenKeyCache.Refresh()` is only called on startup. When key rotation ran at 00:00 UTC and replaced the signing keys, the cache still held the old public keys. All tokens issued after rotation fail validation.

Fix: add a cache TTL (suggest 5min) and hook the rotation event to trigger an async refresh. PR incoming.

messages.text: While user_beta works on the fix - mitigation option: restart auth-service pods to reload keys from disk. Will cause ~30s of degraded availability but clears the stale cache. Shall I proceed?

text.text: While user_beta works on the fix - mitigation option: restart auth-service pods to reload keys from disk. Will cause ~30s of degraded availability but clears the stale cache. Shall I proceed?

attachments.title: RECOVERED: auth-service error rate back to normal
attachments.text: Error rate: 0.4% (was 8.3%). Recovery time: 12 minutes. Pods restarted: 3. Root cause: stale JWT key cache after rotation.

messages.text:
:mega: *Standup update* (I know it's an incident day but let's keep it going)

*Yesterday:* Reviewed PR #828 (rate limiting), added benchmark tests for the new Redis integration.
*Today:* Postmortem writeup for the JWT key cache incident, then back to PR #822 (N+1 query fix).
*Blockers:* None right now, but will need a staging DB refresh to validate the batch query changes.

:mega: *Standup*
*Yesterday:* Hotfix PR #821 (token refresh race condition). Also opened #840 and #841 for the JWT validation bypass issues we found during the incident postmortem.
*Today:* Get #840 and #841 reviewed and merged ASAP. Will need at least 2 reviewers for the security fixes.
*Blockers:* Need someone to prioritize review on the security PRs — they're on `hotfix/auth-regression`.

text.text: :ci-fail: CI failed on PR #826 *feat: dark mode support for dashboard*

2 checks failed:
• `test-frontend` — snapshot mismatch in 3 components
• `a11y-check` — contrast ratio below WCAG AA on dark background for secondary text

messages.text: Yeah that's on me - the dark mode charts use #6B7280 on #1F2937 which is only 3.2:1. Need at least 4.5:1 for WCAG AA. Will fix and push.

messages.text:
:mega: *Standup*
*Yesterday:* Restarted auth-service pods during incident. Reviewed PRs #827 (WebSocket memory leak) and #832 (session timeout propagation) — both LGTM, left comments.
*Today:* Review security PRs #840–#844 on hotfix branch. After that: investigating the DB timeout spike we saw last week (separate from today's auth incident).
*Blockers:* Waiting on user_gamma for staging DB refresh so I can reproduce the timeout issue.

messages.text: :pagerduty: *RESOLVED* - Incident #INC-2847 `auth-service: JWT validation failures` has been resolved. Duration: 18 minutes. Responders: user_alpha, user_beta, user_gamma, user_delta, user_epsilon.

text.text: :pagerduty: *RESOLVED* - Incident #INC-2847 `auth-service: JWT validation failures` has been resolved.

*Duration:* 18 minutes
*Responders:* user_alpha, user_beta, user_gamma, user_delta, user_epsilon
*Root cause:* Stale JWT public key cache after scheduled key rotation
*Resolution:* Pod restart to clear in-memory cache; permanent fix in review (PR #821)

messages.text:
Good work everyone. Quick debrief:
1. Root cause: `TokenKeyCache` only refreshed on startup — fixed in #821 (already merged)
2. Additional JWT security issues found: PRs #840 and #841 open on `hotfix/auth-regression` — *please review today*
3. Postmortem doc: https://www.notion.so/acme/INC-2847-JWT-Cache-Postmortem — @usr_gamma is leading
4. Status page updated to 'Resolved'

This could have been much worse. The JWT bypass (#840) is critical and unrelated to today's incident — good catch @usr_beta.

text.text: Good work everyone. Quick debrief:

4. Status page updated to 'Resolved'
This could have been much worse. The JWT bypass (#840) is critical and unrelated to today's incident — good catch @usr_beta.

messages.text:
Postmortem doc is live: https://www.notion.so/acme/INC-2847-JWT-Cache-Postmortem

Timeline, impact (estimated 2,400 failed auth requests over 18 min), contributing factors, and action items are all drafted. Action items:
- [ ] Add TTL to `TokenKeyCache` (owner: user_beta, due: today)
- [ ] Add cache invalidation hook on key rotation event (owner: user_beta, due: this week)
- [ ] Add alert for key cache staleness > 5min (owner: user_delta, due: this week)
- [ ] Audit other in-memory caches for similar issues (owner: user_gamma, due: sprint end)
- [ ] Review and merge #840 and #841 (owner: all, due: today)
